description:
Python dictionary-based sentiment analysis. Includes Harvard IV-4 and
Loughran and McDonald Financial Statement dictionaries.
name: pysentiment

description: Data pipelines for humans. Work with variables, sources, and pipelines rather than raw data. Also includes high-level tools to analyze, summarize, and transform data.
display_name: Python Tools for Working with Data

name: pd-utils

description:
Create LaTeX documents using only Python. Rather than building a direct Python API to LaTeX, this package
has its own, simpler API to creating documents. It is focused on creating professional-looking documents
with little styling effort. It currently supports documents, presentations, graphics, letters, and resumes. All my papers, presentations, and even my CV are generated using py-ex-latex.
logo_svg_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<!-- Created with Inkscape (http://www.inkscape.org/) -->

<svg
   xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"
   xmlns:cc="http://creativecommons.org/ns#"

name: py-ex-latex

description:
High-level tools for running regressions. Handles fixed effects, 2+ way clustering, hypothesis testing,
lagged variables, differenced variables, interaction effects, iteration tools, and producing summaries for a
variety of models including OLS, Logit, Probit, Quantile, and Fama-Macbeth.

name: regtools

description:
py-file-conf is a Python framework for flow-based programming and managing configuration. To use it, you register
your main functions and classes, and config files are created automatically for them. It provides a way to run these functions
individually, in a list, in sections, or a combination thereof. Configuration can be 
dynamically updated, enabling powerful scripting.

name: py-file-conf

description:
This is package includes Mixin classes which may be added to your own project classes to add
certain functionality to them.

name: py-mixins

description: Quick start a Python package hosted on PyPi with Sphinx auto documentation hosted on Github pages
name: pypi-sphinx-quickstart

description:
Use this tool to drive Excel using the Thompson Reuters Eikon plugin to download Datastream data. Useful
for downloading large amounts of data.
name: datastream-excel-downloader-py

description:
A tool used to work with Excel from Python. It currently mainly handles starting and stopping
Excel, and getting the active Excel instance and workbook so that COM commands can be run on them.
name: py-excel-driver

description:
A tool to drive Excel using the Capital IQ plugin to download Capital IQ data. Useful for downloading
large data sets from Capital IQ.
name: capiq-excel-downloader-py

description:
Use this tool to select files of given file types in a folder, and track whether these files have been processed,
regardless of whether the script needs to be run multiple times. Stores progress on the files as a text file in the
same folder, so that a long-running operation on many files can be resumed where it left off if it was stopped.
It will also automatically estimate time to completion.

and outputs reports. This package helped generate this list of software projects.

name: project-report

description:
A Python SDK for the CryptoCompare APIs which require an API key. Allows
downloading price history, social information, and blockchain information
for cryptoassets.
name: cryptocompare-py

description:
This is a GUI, Python, CLI tool for splitting repos. If you have a part of a repo which
you would now like to be a separate repo, and you would like to separate the history
of the two repos such that the new repo contains commits referencing its files and
any references to those files in the old repo have been removed, then this is
the tool for you.

name: repo-splitter

description:
Work with financial statement data in Python. Can calculate free cash flows and help project
financial statements, automatically balancing the balance sheet.

name: py-finstmt

description: Github Action for getting the latest version of a PyPI package.
name: pypi-latest-version-action

description:
A template to use for starting a new Python package
    which is hosted on PyPi and uses Sphinx for documentation
    hosted on Github pages. It has a built-in CI/CD system using Github Actions.
name: cookiecutter-pypi-sphinx

display_name: Copier Template for a PyPI package documented with Sphinx and updated with Flexlate
name: copier-pypi-sphinx-flexlate

description: Github Action for checking whether a Github issue already exists.
name: check-if-issue-exists-action

description: Generate BibTeX reference objects from DOIs and strings

name: bibtex-gen

description:
A very simple API to storing Python objects built on ZODB. Use the .store method to store an object at a path,
and .get to retrieve an object from a path.

name: obj-cache

Python sensitivity analysis - run models with varying inputs to produce 
visualizations including gradient DataFrames and hex-bin plots
